brief.query: Generate and validate a vtk.js slice scene with browser evidence vtk.js Slice Benchmark The candidate should preserve a deterministic slice-oriented render path and browser validation contract.
recommendedPatterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval.
Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict.
Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation.
Keep slice scenes explicit about image data, mapper setup, and viewport assumptions.
Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order.
failureFixPairs: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable.
If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command.
If slice rendering regresses, compare mapper wiring and viewport setup against a minimal slice baseline.
If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first.
If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts.
When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic.
brief.generationPrompt: Generate vtk.js code for the requested scene while preserving a deterministic browser-validation path. Scene kind: slice. Use these retrieval-backed patterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval. | Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict. | Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation. | Keep slice scenes explicit about image data, mapper setup, and viewport assumptions. | Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order. | Always call renderer.resetCamera() before renderWindow.render().. Avoid regressions captured by these failure-fix hints: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable. | If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command. | If slice rendering regresses, compare mapper wiring and viewport setup against a minimal slice baseline. | If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first. | If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts. | When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic.. Acceptance contract: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification. | The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render(). | Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png. | The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors. | The generated slice workflow should make image-data and slice-mapper setup explicit..
acceptanceChecks: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors.
The generated slice workflow should make image-data and slice-mapper setup explicit.

generatedCode.summary: vtk.js generated candidate for slice scene

starterNotes: This generated candidate keeps a deterministic browser-validation path by default.
The browser script is ready for Phase 5 render verification without additional bundling steps.
The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
